entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A dryland legume crop targeted for improvement to ensure food and nutrition security in Africa.

entities.name: DRYLAND LEGUMES
entities.description: Legumes suitable for diversification of farming systems as insurance crops to ensure productivity.

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Endemic stresses that negatively impact crop productivity.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output crucial for meeting food demands of a growing population.

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITION SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring reliable access to sufficient, nutrient-dense food.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing productivity by delivering high yielding and resilient varieties.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The ability of a crop variety to produce a large amount of harvestable product.

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand and recover from environmental stresses.

entities.name: NUTRIENT DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of beneficial nutrients in a crop to address global nutrition agendas.

entities.name: MOLECULAR TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technological advancements used for genome sequencing, genetic mapping, and QTL identification.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT GENOME
entities.description: The complete set of genetic material of the groundnut plant.

entities.name: GENETIC MAPS
entities.description: Representations of the relative positions of genes or markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI (QTLS)
entities.description: Regions of DNA associated with specific quantitative traits of importance.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The region where groundnut improvement and farming system integration are targeted.

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic makeup of an organism, now available at lower costs.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used in breeding to predict the breeding value of individuals based on genomic markers.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of changing the traits of plants to produce desired characteristics and variety release.

entities.name: WILD GENETIC RESOURCE BASE
entities.description: The genetic diversity found in wild relatives of cultivated crops used to broaden the genetic base.

entities.name: VARIETY RELEASE
entities.description: The process of making new, improved crop varieties available for agricultural use.

entities.name: MOLECULAR TOOLS
entities.description: Tools derived from molecular biology used to enhance breeding efficiency.

entities.name: WIDE HYBRIDIZATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Breeding methods involving crossing distantly related species or varieties.

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Rapid and automated measurement of plant traits to support breeding programs.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: POVERTY ALLEVIATION
entities.description: The reduction of poverty through improved agricultural productivity and food security.

entities.name: OMICS
entities.description: A collective term for high-throughput biological technologies used to leverage resources and expertise.
